title: Collector salespersons and credit agents
description: Organize activities to raise funds or otherwise solicit and gather monetary donations or other gifts for an organization. May design and produce promotional materials. May also raise awareness of the organization's work, goals, and financial needs. Review individual applications for insurance to evaluate degree of risk involved and determine acceptance of applications. Directly supervise and coordinate activities of sales workers other than retail sales workers. May perform duties such as budgeting, accounting, and personnel work, in addition to supervisory duties. (Based on 12 related US occupations.)

tasks.task: Call on policyholders to deliver and explain policy, analyze insurance program, suggest changes, or change beneficiaries.
Calculate premiums and establish payment methods for insurance policies.
Attend community events, meetings, or conferences to promote organizational goals or solicit donations.
Contact corporate representatives, government officials, or community leaders to increase awareness of organizational needs or activities.
Create or update donor databases and develop strategies to encourage contributions.
Design or produce promotional materials to market or advertise fundraising events.
Develop fundraising activity plans that maximize participation or contributions and minimize costs.
Direct or supervise fundraising staff, including volunteers, and monitor budgets or financial data for fundraising organizations.
Secure commitments of participation or donations from individuals or corporate donors and identify potential donors.
Prepare reports or presentations to communicate fundraising program data and write letters of thanks to donors.
Locate and notify customers of delinquent accounts and confer with them to determine reasons for overdue payments and necessary actions for debt repayment.
Examine documents to determine degree of risk and interview prospective clients to collect data for insurance evaluations.
Develop marketing strategies to compete in the insurance industry and create marketing materials that convey information about services.
Provide customers with information about products, prices, availability, and assist in selecting products based on their needs and specifications.
Identify prospective customers using directories, leads, or participation in organizations and trade shows.
Stock or distribute resources, such as samples or promotional or educational materials.
Answer customers' questions about products, prices, availability, or credit terms.
Quote prices, credit terms, contract terms, or fulfillment dates for services.
Monitor market conditions, product innovations, competitors' products, prices, and sales.
Perform administrative duties, such as preparing sales budgets and reports, keeping sales records, and filing expense account reports.
Obtain credit information about prospective customers and check stock levels, reordering merchandise as necessary.
Hire, train, and evaluate personnel, while monitoring sales staff performance to ensure that goals are met.
Directly supervise and coordinate employees engaged in sales, inventory-taking, and reconciling cash receipts.
Confer with company officials to develop methods and procedures to increase sales, expand markets, and promote business.
Prepare sales and inventory reports for management and budget departments, and keep records pertaining to purchases and sales.
Plan and prepare work schedules, and assign employees to specific duties, providing assistance in performing difficult tasks.
Attend company meetings to exchange product information and coordinate work activities with other departments.
Coordinate sales promotion activities, such as preparing merchandise displays and advertising copy, and negotiate with retail merchants to improve product exposure.
Analyze details of sales territories to assess their growth potential and to set quotas, examining products for resale to determine condition.
Visit retailers and sales representatives to promote products and gather information.

Calculate costs of goods or services.
Prepare drawings or diagrams of products or services.
Develop proposals for current or prospective customers.
Discuss account status or activity with customers or patrons.
Customize energy products or services to meet customer needs.
Examine condition of property or products.
Monitor inventories of products or materials.
Obtain personal or financial information about customers or applicants.
Interview employees, customers, or others to collect information.
Assess locations for potential green technology installations.
Assess the cost effectiveness of products, projects, or services.
Evaluate potential of products, technologies, or resources.
Review accuracy of sales or other transactions.
Examine documents to verify adherence to requirements.
Maintain data in information systems or databases.
Communicate organizational information to customers or other stakeholders.
Share sales-related or market information with colleagues.
Provide notifications to customers or patrons.
Deliver promotional presentations to current or prospective customers.
Respond to customer problems or complaints.
Manage information technology projects or system activities.
Coordinate logistics or other business operations.
Advise others on business or operational matters.
Assign duties or work schedules to employees.

emerging_tasks.task: Answer agents' questions about insurance coverage.
Contact customers to ensure their satisfaction with products or services.
Log client information and sales leads into tracking documents or software.
Train new recruits or other employees.
